Description:
*SOS we lost some binary sir!*

Solution:
My favorite tool to use when dealing with cryptography is **CyberChef**.
Link: https://gchq.github.io/CyberChef/ (You can download the offline version for speed but it lacks very few features that depends on online)

When we open **Lost_Some_Binary.txt** we see:

```
01001000 01101001 01101001 01101001 00100000 01101101 01100001 01101110 00101100 01101000 01101111 01110111 00100000 01110010 00100000 01110101 00100000 00111111 01001001 01110011 00100000 01101001 01110100 00100000 00111010 00101001 00101001 00101001 00101001 01010010 01100001 01110111 01110010 00101101 01011110 01011110 01011011 01011101 00100000 00100000 01001100 01010011 01000010 01111011 00111110 00111100 01111101 00100001 01001100 01010011 01000010 01111110 01111110 01001100 01010011 01000010 01111110 01111110 00101101 00101101 00101101 01110110 00110001 01110100 00100000 00100000 01111011 00110001 00110011 00110101 00111001 00110000 00110000 01011111 00110001 00110011 00110011 00110111 00110000 01111101
```

Which is clearly binary as it states in the challenge and from our interpretation, so we use **CyberChef** to convert it to human-readable format.

![](images/Pasted%20image%2020251102223252.png)

Copy the binary in 1 -> Search for Binary in 2 -> Look at the output in 3

The output:
```
Hiii man,how r u ?Is it :))))Rawr-^^[]  LSB{><}!LSB~~LSB~~---v1t  {135900_13370}
```

We clearly see a "LSB" hint, which refers to Least Significant bit.
All you have to do is take the last bit from the left or first from the right from each binary group then regroup them again.

Result:
```
01110110 00110001 01110100 01111011 01001100 01010011 01000010 00111010 00111110 01111101
```

Put it in **CyberChef** and you get:
![](images/Pasted%20image%2020251102225323.png)

You do it manually but you may make mistakes so here is a script:

```python
data = "01001000 01101001 01101001 01101001 00100000 01101101 01100001 01101110 00101100 01101000 01101111 01110111 00100000 01110010 00100000 01110101 00100000 00111111 01001001 01110011 00100000 01101001 01110100 00100000 00111010 00101001 00101001 00101001 00101001 01010010 01100001 01110111 01110010 00101101 01011110 01011110 01011011 01011101 00100000 00100000 01001100 01010011 01000010 01111011 00111110 00111100 01111101 00100001 01001100 01010011 01000010 01111110 01111110 01001100 01010011 01000010 01111110 01111110 00101101 00101101 00101101 01110110 00110001 01110100 00100000 00100000 01111011 00110001 00110011 00110101 00111001 00110000 00110000 01011111 00110001 00110011 00110011 00110111 00110000 01111101"

bits = data.split()
bytes_vals = [int(b,2) for b in bits]
lsb_bits = ''.join(str(b & 1) for b in bytes_vals)

chars = []
for i in range(0, len(lsb_bits) - (len(lsb_bits) % 8), 8):
    byte = lsb_bits[i:i+8]
    chars.append(chr(int(byte, 2)))
result = ''.join(chars)
print("LSB bitstream ->", result)
```

Flag:
**v1t{LSB:>}**
